courses.title: Research Methods in Asian American Studies
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Introduction to qualitative community-based research in Asian American communities, including theory, data collection, analysis, and presentation.

courses.title: Biracial and Multiracial Identity
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Interdisciplinary study of multiracial identities, racial structures, and social and legal systems of racial classification.

courses.title: Asian American Communities: Field Practicum
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
preparatory: Concurrent enrollment in AAS 345 or other related course
courses.description: Fieldwork and seminar focusing on community institutions, including supervised experience and service learning.

courses.title: Urbanization and Asian American Communities
preparatory: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Study of the development of Asian American communities in the U.S. from 1850 to present, with focus on urban Southern California.

courses.title: Asian American Families
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Overview of demographic, cultural, and historical aspects of Asian American families in the U.S.

courses.title: Asian American Sexuality
preparatory: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Explores identities and issues in Asian American sexuality, including gender roles, media, and social structures.

courses.title: Introduction to the African Diaspora
courses.description: Students explore historical, theoretical, and cultural approaches to the African Diaspora across Africa, the Americas, and Europe, including identity formation and social movements.

courses.title: History of African Civilizations
courses.description: Survey of major African societies from early human history to the 19th century, including leadership, culture, and social organization.

courses.title: The Black Aesthetic
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: In-depth exploration of African worldview through Black aesthetic expressions such as art, music, poetry, and spirituality.

courses.title: Diversity and Public Policy
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
preparatory: Introductory level course in history and government (Title 5)
courses.description: Examines how race, gender, class, and sexuality influence public policy, including issues like voting rights, welfare, criminal justice, and equity.

courses.title: African American Social Movements
courses.description: Study of African American social movements including Civil Rights and Black Power, along with global activism and historical context.

courses.title: The Black Child
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Explores African American childhood development, socialization, and achievement.

courses.title: Equity and Diversity in Schools
courses.description: Examines educational equity, diversity, curriculum, and bias in schools. Not available for credit in addition to AAS 417, ARMN 417, CHS 417 or ELPS 417.

courses.title: The Central American Diaspora
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Comparative survey of the socioeconomic conditions and cultural life of Central American peoples in the U.S., particularly in California.

courses.title: Fieldwork in Central American Communities
courses.description: Field study in a selected Central American community with supervised placement and reflection.

courses.title: Fieldwork in Barrio Studies
courses.description: Fieldwork focused on social change in Chicana/o/Latina/o communities, including community engagement and reflection.

courses.title: The Chicana/o in Films
preparatory: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Analysis of the representation of Chicana/o communities in film and documentaries.

courses.title: Pre-Cuauhtemoc Meso-American Civilizations
courses.description: Survey of Meso-American civilizations prior to 1519, focusing on central Mexico.

courses.title: Chicanas/os and the Media
courses.description: Analysis of portrayals of Chicanas/os in various media forms.

courses.title: Equity and Diversity in Schools
courses.description: Examines educational equity, diversity, and bias in schools.

courses.title: The Chicana/o Child
courses.description: Study of development and achievement of Chicana/o and Latina/o children.

courses.title: The Chicana/o Adolescent
preparatory: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Explores adolescent identity and development among Chicana/o and Latina/o youth.

courses.title: Counseling the Chicana/o Child
preparatory: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Examines psychological and educational factors affecting Chicana/o children.

courses.title: Current Educational Theories of Chicana/os in the Schools
courses.description: Review of research on issues affecting Chicano students in schools and teacher responses, including classroom observation.

courses.title: Chicana/o Public History
courses.description: Explores how public historical narratives are created and interpreted, focusing on Chicana/o perspectives.

courses.title: Theory and the Chicana/o Experience
courses.description: Study of political, social, and cultural theories related to the Chicana/o experience in the U.S.

courses.title: Politics of the Chicana/o
courses.description: Critical study of Chicana/o political issues, participation, and structures in the U.S.

courses.title: Environmental Justice and Chicana/o Communities
courses.description: Examines environmental inequality and its impact on Chicana/o and other communities.

courses.title: The Chicana/o and Social Institutions
preparatory: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Study of interactions between Chicano communities and dominant social institutions.

courses.title: Chicana/o-Latina/o Children’s Literature in Communities
preparatory: Completion of a lower division literature course or CHS 380
courses.description: Critical analysis and application of Chicana/o-Latina/o children’s literature in community and school settings.

courses.title: Nahuatl I
courses.description: Introduction to Classical Nahuatl language including phonology, syntax, and translation.

courses.title: Critical Thinking About Research Writing
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Focuses on composing, reading, and analyzing research writing with emphasis on critical thinking.

courses.title: Genetics and Society
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Non-technical introduction to genetics and its applications in society.
blocks: GE Area 5 Physical and Biological Sciences

courses.title: Adventure Recreation and Human Relations and Lab
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Explores cultural identity, diversity, and personal growth through experiential learning.
blocks: GE Section F Comparative Cultural Studies

courses.title: Perspectives of Dance and Lab
prerequisites: Completion of the lower division writing requirement
courses.description: Study of dance as performance, culture, and artistic expression with lab participation.
